concept: Imagine a fighting game where combatants are composite beings assembled from interchangeable parts that can be strategically detached and reattached during battle. Limbs function independently when separated, allowing simultaneous attacks from multiple positions, but overall effectiveness diminishes with each detached component. Environmental hazards can damage or enhance detached parts, creating risk/reward scenarios for strategic dismemberment. Advanced techniques involve temporarily reconfiguring your assembly in unconventional ways to enable special moves impossible in standard formation. Tournament champions are masters of asymmetrical fighting - strategically sacrificing certain components to create overwhelming advantages with their remaining specialized parts.
